name: United Kingdom (Royal Standard)

colors.symbolism: the fields of the English and Irish quarters
the gold (or) of the heraldic charges
the field of the Irish harp quarter
the silver strings of the Irish harp

description: A banner of the Royal Arms divided into four quarters: 1st and 4th, a red field with three gold lions passant guardant (England); 2nd, a gold field with a red lion rampant within a double tressure flory-counter-flory (Scotland); 3rd, a blue field with a gold harp strung silver (Ireland).
history: The Royal Standard of the United Kingdom in its current quartered form has been used since 1837, when Queen Victoria removed the Hanoverian inescutcheon on her accession. It is the personal flag of the sovereign and is flown only when the monarch is present or in residence; it is never flown at half-mast.

features.symbolism: a banner of the Royal Arms combining the historic arms of England, Scotland and Ireland

features.role: Three lions passant guardant of England (1st and 4th quarters)
Lion rampant within a double tressure flory-counter-flory of Scotland (2nd quarter)
Gold harp of Ireland, stringed silver (3rd quarter)
